---
title: "User's Guide — Document Generation & Image Pipeline"
subtitle: "End-to-end: prompts.json → Gemini Nano Banana → PNGs → DOCX / PPTX / PDF / EPUB"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Production-grade walkthrough for the UIAO document-generation pipeline. Covers Gemini API setup, prompts authoring, image generation, document assembly, and operational guardrails."
aspirational: true
---

The UIAO Modernization Atlas ships with a **deterministic document-generation pipeline** that turns a prompts specification into a set of rendered publication binaries (DOCX, PPTX, PDF, EPUB) with AI-generated images embedded at the correct positions. This page is the canonical user's guide for running that pipeline end-to-end.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Requires a Gemini API key
The image-generation step calls Google's Gemini API (specifically the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model, also known as **Nano Banana**). You need an active key stored in the `GEMINI_API_KEY` environment variable. Never commit a key to source — see [§ Key management](#key-management) below.
:::

## The pipeline, in one picture

```
prompts.json              (you author this)
    │
    ▼
generate_images.py        (calls Gemini; writes PNGs)
    │
    ▼
docs/images/*.png         (review, accept, or regenerate)
    │
    ▼
build.py                  (assembles publication)
    │
    ▼
UIAO-<Publication>.docx + .pptx + .pdf + .epub
```

Each stage is **idempotent**, **inspectable**, and **replayable**. Images persist across runs so a second build doesn't re-spend API calls unless you force regeneration.

## The two scripts

| Script | Purpose | When you run it |
|---|---|---|
| [`docs/generate_images.py`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/docs/generate_images.py) | Unified image pipeline: harvests `[IMAGE-NN:]` / `[IMAGE-REF:]` placeholders across the repo, calls Gemini Nano Banana, writes PNGs + prompt-hash sidecars, resolves canonical `[IMAGE-REF:]` lookups against `src/uiao/canon/image-registry.yaml`. Also supports the legacy `docs/prompts.json` flat-list input. | You've added or edited prompts and want the PNGs regenerated. Cache-gated by `prompt_sha256`; unchanged prompts are skipped. |
| [`docs/publications/01-executive-brief/build.py`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/docs/publications/01-executive-brief/build.py) | Full publication assembler: generates images (optional), inserts into DOCX, produces all output formats | You're cutting a publication release |

::: {.callout-note title="Legacy scripts retired"}
The older `docs/generate_scuba_images.py` and `scripts/generate_scuba_images.py`
generators were retired once `docs/generate_images.py` absorbed per-doc
IMAGE-PROMPTS.md walking and canonical registry resolution. If you see
references to them in old session logs or ADRs, treat those as historical.
:::

Both scripts are **env-variable-driven** and **path-self-locating** — they work from any clone, any OS, any user. No hardcoded paths.

## Prerequisites

### Tools

| Dependency | Install | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| **Python 3.10+** | `python --version` to check | All 3 scripts |
| **google-genai** | `pip install google-genai` | Image generation |
| **Pillow** | `pip install Pillow` | Image manipulation in `build.py` |
| **python-pptx** | `pip install python-pptx` | PPTX output in `build.py` |
| **ebooklib** | `pip install ebooklib` | EPUB output in `build.py` |
| **lxml** | `pip install lxml` | DOCX XML edits in `build.py` |
| **LibreOffice** | `sudo apt install libreoffice` / `brew install libreoffice` / installer on Windows | PDF conversion in `build.py` |

Or install all at once:

```bash
pip install google-genai Pillow python-pptx ebooklib lxml
```

### Accounts

- **Google AI Studio account** with an active project (`gen-lang-client-<id>`) and an API key created at [https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys](https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys).
- Tier 1 billing is sufficient for normal authoring volumes. The `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model has a free quota; exceeding it requires billing enabled.

## Key management

The scripts read exactly **one** environment variable: **`GEMINI_API_KEY`**.

### For local workstation use (one-time setup)

**Windows PowerShell (persistent, user-level):**

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("GEMINI_API_KEY", "<paste-key>", "User")
# Open a fresh PowerShell to pick it up, then verify:
echo $env:GEMINI_API_KEY
```

**macOS / Linux (persistent, in your shell profile):**

```bash
# Bash — add to ~/.bashrc
export GEMINI_API_KEY="<paste-key>"

# Zsh — add to ~/.zshrc
export GEMINI_API_KEY="<paste-key>"

# Verify in a fresh shell:
echo $GEMINI_API_KEY
```

### For CI (GitHub Actions)

Store at **Repository Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret**:

- **Name:** `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- **Secret:** (paste the key value)

Workflows reference it as `${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}`. No workflow currently calls the image-gen scripts; add one if/when you need batched CI generation.

### Hard rules

- ❌ **Never** hardcode the key in any source file. PR #76 rotated out the first leak; PR review gates should catch any reintroduction.
- ❌ **Never** paste the key into a PR body, issue, commit message, or chat you're willing to share.
- ✅ Password manager (1Password, Bitwarden, KeePass) is the canonical home.
- ✅ Rotate if you suspect exposure (free at [https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys](https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys)).

## Stage 1 — Authoring prompts

Each publication (or series) maintains a JSON file naming the images it needs and the prompts that generate them. Default locations:

- `docs/prompts.json` — legacy flat-list prompts consumed by `generate_images.py` (still supported for drop-in bulk runs)
- `<doc-folder>/IMAGE-PROMPTS.md` — per-document prompts, one file per owning `.qmd`/`.md`. The modern authoring pattern — placeholders in the prose (`[IMAGE-NN: ...]`) drive everything.
- `docs/publications/<publication>/prompts.json` — publication-scoped prompts consumed by that publication's `build.py`

### Schema

Each file is a JSON array of objects, each object being one image:

```json
[
  {
    "filename": "address-governance-flow.png",
    "prompt": "A federal agency network administrator at a terminal, surrounded by glowing IPAM records flowing between authoritative directories and DHCP leases, drawn in the UIAO narrative-comic style with soft governmental blues and yellows. The operator's face shows quiet focus; the records are stylized to suggest policy provenance.",
    "width": 1024,
    "height": 1024,
    "retries": 3
  },
  {
    "filename": "drift-detection-loop.png",
    "prompt": "A closed-loop diagram depicted as a flowing river of signals — scan, classify, remediate — with a human-scale operator figure standing in the center, calm, observing.",
    "width": 1024,
    "height": 1024,
    "retries": 3
  }
]
```

**Required keys** per entry:

- `filename` — output PNG name (relative to `docs/images/`)
- `prompt` — the generation prompt (natural language; vendor constraints apply)

**Optional keys**:

- `width`, `height` — default 1024×1024 (Gemini Nano Banana sweet spot)
- `retries` — number of attempts per prompt if the first response is flagged/truncated (default: 3)

### Prompt authoring principles

1. **Be specific about style.** The UIAO narrative comics (`docs/publications/series-assets/UIAO-*.jpg`) are the canonical style anchor — soft governmental blues and yellows, human-scale operators, restrained typography, policy-provenance aesthetic.
2. **Name the subject, the setting, and the feeling.** Nano Banana follows explicit composition cues more reliably than impressionistic ones.
3. **Avoid logos, real people, or proprietary marks.** Gemini's safety filters will block these and you'll waste an API call.
4. **Test one prompt before bulk-generating.** The `--dry-run` flag (below) prints prompts without calling the API.

## Stage 2 — Running image generation

From the repo root:

```bash
# Set the key (once per shell session, if not persisted)
export GEMINI_API_KEY="<your-key>"

# Walk every IMAGE-PROMPTS.md + prompts.json in the repo, regenerate only
# what changed (cache-gated on prompt_sha256).
python docs/generate_images.py

# Dry-run (prints prompts, no API calls, no cost)
python docs/generate_images.py --dry-run

# Scan only (report placeholders; no generation)
python docs/generate_images.py --scan-only
```

Output:

- PNGs written to `docs/images/` (or the `OUTPUT_DIR` override)
- One line of stdout per prompt, naming the file and the result (success / retry / failure)
- A 2-second delay between prompts to stay well under Gemini's rate limits

### What to expect

- **~3-8 seconds per image** at current Gemini Nano Banana speeds
- **Transient failures retry automatically** per the `retries` key in the prompt entry
- **Permanent failures** (safety-filter blocks, quota exhaustion) print a red error and skip to the next prompt — rerun after adjusting the prompt or upgrading billing

### Force-regeneration

Images persist across runs. If you want to re-run a specific prompt (e.g., the style didn't land), delete the PNG from `docs/images/` and rerun. Or change the `filename` in `prompts.json` so the new output doesn't collide.

## Stage 3 — Reviewing and accepting images

Generated PNGs land in `docs/images/`. Before committing:

1. **Open each new PNG.** Does the composition match the canonical comic style? Does it communicate the abstraction?
2. **Delete rejects.** A single `docs/images/<name>.png` is the canonical artifact; don't commit draft variants.
3. **Size-check.** If an image exceeds ~2 MB, consider regenerating with a prompt that produces simpler composition — large files inflate the repo and slow GitHub Pages.
4. **Confirm filename matches the consuming document.** `build.py` and the book article `.qmd` pages reference specific filenames (see the book's `images/article-NN.png` convention).

When satisfied, commit the PNGs in a single PR scoped to the publication or article they support.

## Stage 4 — Document assembly (the `build.py` pipeline)

`docs/publications/01-executive-brief/build.py` is the reference publication assembler. Other publications can copy the pattern.

### Usage

```bash
cd docs/publications/01-executive-brief/

# Full pipeline — generate images + all output formats
python build.py

# Skip image generation (use images already in docs/images/)
python build.py --skip-images

# Only specific output formats
python build.py --formats docx pdf

# Dry-run (print the plan, no action)
python build.py --dry-run
```

### What `build.py` does

1. Reads `prompts.json` (sibling to the script).
2. If `--skip-images` is NOT set: calls `generate_images(...)` with the environment's `GEMINI_API_KEY`, writing PNGs to `docs/images/`.
3. Opens `UIAO-Executive-Brief-SOURCE.docx` (the authoring source).
4. Inserts each image at its designated position (images are 4" × 4", 914400 EMU = 1 inch).
5. Writes `UIAO-Executive-Brief.docx` with images embedded.
6. Converts DOCX → PPTX (via `python-pptx`).
7. Converts DOCX → PDF (via LibreOffice subprocess — requires LibreOffice installed).
8. Converts DOCX → EPUB (via `ebooklib`).

### Outputs

In the same directory as `build.py`:

- `UIAO-Executive-Brief.docx` — the canonical published form
- `UIAO-Executive-Brief.pptx` — briefing deck
- `UIAO-Executive-Brief.pdf` — print-ready / auditor-ready
- `UIAO-Executive-Brief.epub` — ebook reader / accessibility

These are **committed binaries** for agency readers who don't have Quarto or Python installed. The Quarto-rendered HTML remains the website canon; these formats are derivatives.

## Stage 5 — Quarto rendering (orthogonal path)

The main Modernization Atlas site is **Quarto-rendered** from `.qmd` files in `docs/`, independent of the DOCX/PPTX pipeline above. Quarto consumes images via markdown image references (`![alt](images/foo.png)`) — the PNGs from Stage 3 are reused.

```bash
cd docs/
quarto render --to html    # renders the full site to _site/
```

CI runs this automatically on every PR via `.github/workflows/quarto.yml`. The site deploys to `https://whalermike.github.io/uiao/` on push to `main`.

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `Error: GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable not set` | The env var isn't exported in this shell | `export GEMINI_API_KEY=...` (or re-open PowerShell if you set it at User level) |
| `google.api_core.exceptions.PermissionDenied: 403` | Key is valid but the model / project isn't authorized | Enable `Generative Language API` in the GCP console for your project |
| `API_KEY_INVALID` | Key is dead (rotated, revoked, or mistyped) | Generate a new key; update the env var / Actions secret |
| `SafetyError: PROHIBITED_CONTENT` | Prompt triggered Gemini's safety filter | Rewrite prompt to remove restricted content (logos, real people, political framing, weapons, etc.) |
| `Quota exceeded` | Free tier hit; you're on Postpay but haven't enabled billing | Enable billing at `https://aistudio.google.com/billing` |
| `PDF conversion failed` | LibreOffice not installed or not on PATH | Install LibreOffice; verify `soffice --version` on PATH |
| Image inserted at wrong position in DOCX | Filename in `prompts.json` doesn't match the placeholder in `SOURCE.docx` | Either rename the PNG or edit the placeholder in the source DOCX |

## Security checklist (before pushing new content)

- [ ] `grep -r 'AIzaSy' .` in your working tree returns zero matches (or only ADR-025 historical mentions)
- [ ] `GEMINI_API_KEY` is NOT in any file you're about to commit (including `.env`)
- [ ] `.gitignore` includes `.env` and any local credential files
- [ ] Generated PNGs do not contain embedded EXIF with PII (strip with `exiftool -all= *.png` if in doubt)
- [ ] No prompts in `prompts.json` reference real people, real agency logos, or names that would cause a safety filter block OR a political/legal issue if the image leaks

## Automation (future)

A GitHub Actions workflow for on-demand image generation could be added at `.github/workflows/image-gen.yml`:

```yaml
name: Generate images (on-demand)

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      prompts_file:
        description: "Path to prompts.json (relative to repo root)"
        required: true
        default: "docs/prompts.json"

jobs:
  generate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install google-genai Pillow
      - run: python docs/generate_images.py
        env:
          GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: generated-images
          path: docs/images/
```

**Not yet wired.** Ship this workflow when:

1. `prompts.json` files exist (currently absent from the repo)
2. You want reproducible CI-driven regeneration
3. You want the GitHub Actions secret you already set to actually be used

## Cross-references

- [UIAO_128 Education Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-education.md) — the canon this pipeline supports (comic-led agency onboarding)
- [FINDING-001 FedRAMP-INR](../findings/fedramp-gcc-moderate-informed-network-routing.md) — an example governance finding whose existence the comics help communicate
- [Academy — Contributor tier-1 setup](contributor-tier-1-setup.qmd) — separate pipeline; authentication model is similar (env-var-only, never hardcoded)
- [Academy — Entra ID integration guide](adapters/entra-id.qmd) — reference for the "step-by-step, production-grade, certificate-anchored" guide pattern
- [Gemini API — model reference](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini)
- [Google AI Studio — API keys](https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys)
